title: His Gifts to the Churches, and Bounties to Virgins and to the Poor.

source.title: NPNF2-01. Eusebius Pamphilius: Church History, Life of Constantine, Oration in Praise of Constantine

verses.text: His liberality, however, was most especially exercised on behalf of the churches of God.

In some cases he granted lands, in others he issued supplies of food for the support of the poor, of orphan children, and widows; besides which, he evinced much care and forethought in fully providing the naked and destitute with clothing.
He distinguished, however, with most special honor those who had devoted their lives to the practice of Divine philosophy.

Hence his respect, little short of veneration, for God’s most holy and ever virgin choir: for he felt assured that the God to whom such persons devoted themselves was himself an inmate of their souls.
